description: User's relationship status evolves across six sessions from single to dating to engaged and back to single. Tests tracking of emotionally sensitive mutable facts and whether the system handles retraction gracefully.

messages.content: I've been single for a while now. Any suggestions for weekend activities for someone flying solo?
Plenty of great solo options! Hiking, checking out a new museum exhibit, trying a cooking class, or hitting up a local coffee shop with a good book.
I started seeing someone named Alex about a month ago. Things are going really well.
That's great to hear! Sounds like things are off to a good start with Alex.
Alex and I moved in together last week. We found a place in the Pearl District.
Congrats on the move! The Pearl District is a wonderful area. How's the new place?
Big news: Alex proposed last weekend and I said yes! We're engaged!
Congratulations! That's wonderful news. Have you started thinking about wedding plans?
I need to talk about something tough. Alex and I called off the engagement. We split up about two weeks ago. I moved out.
I'm sorry to hear that. Breakups are really difficult, especially after an engagement. I'm here if you need to talk or want help with anything practical.
I'm trying to rebuild my social life. Can you remind me what you know about my relationship history this year? I want to make sure I'm being honest with a new therapist about the timeline.

previous_values.value: living with Alex in Pearl District

probe.prompt: Can you remind me what you know about my relationship history this year? I want to make sure I'm being honest with a new therapist about the timeline.

interference.description: The breakup and engagement cancellation are emotionally charged; a system might avoid recalling painful details even when explicitly asked
